title: Morning Robotics Scan
report:
## GeoGS-SLAM

### The Breakthrough
The authors introduce GeoGS, a geometry‑only variant of 3D Gaussian splatting that removes colour and opacity parameters, cutting per‑primitive storage by more than 80% and speeding up convergence while improving geometric accuracy on synthetic and real‑world benchmarks.

### The Caveats
The paper never evaluates against existing appearance‑based 3DGS or SLAM baselines, so the claimed superiority is unsubstantiated.

## Dual Latent Memory in Vision‑Language‑Action Models for Robotic Manipulation

### The Breakthrough
LaMem‑VLA embeds past experience as latent memory tokens inside a vision‑language‑action pipeline via four modules—curator, seeker, condenser, and weaver—allowing memory to be interleaved with current observation and instruction, and showing performance gains on SimplerEnv and LIBERO simulators.

### The Caveats
Performance gains are reported without naming any baseline methods, leaving superiority unclear.

## ELEANOR: 85 cm 3D‑printed soft arm inspired by the elephant trunk

### The Breakthrough
ELEANOR is an 85 cm soft continuum arm fabricated with a tapered backbone and dense tendon bundles that replicate longitudinal and oblique muscle actions, enabling whole‑body grasping of objects of varied shapes in qualitative demos.

### The Caveats
Only qualitative demonstrations are provided and no comparison to existing continuum‑robot baselines, so the claimed dexterity and scalability remain unverified.

## EmbodiedGen V2: An Agentic, Simulation‑Ready 3D World Engine for Embodied AI

### The Breakthrough
EmbodiedGen V2 automatically creates simulation‑ready 3D worlds with 96.5% human acceptance and 98.6% collision‑free success, boosting simulation‑to‑real task success from 21.7% to 75.0% in robot experiments.

### The Caveats
The work provides no baseline or prior‑art comparison, making the magnitude of improvement unclear.

## TorchRL: PyTorch‑native reinforcement learning library for robotics and control

### The Breakthrough
TorchRL 0.13 centres on a TensorDict data model and modular components for policies, environments, collectors, and replay buffers, adding Triton‑accelerated recurrent nets, async prioritized replay, and new transforms for robotics and multi‑agent tasks.

### The Caveats
The announcement lacks empirical performance results on robotics or other RL tasks.

## Google Cloud Robotics Core: Open‑source fleet management on Kubernetes

### The Breakthrough
The platform integrates Kubernetes federation with bidirectional robot‑cloud communication, packaging apps as Helm charts and exposing unified APIs for ML, logging, and monitoring, plus Terraform scripts for provisioning.

### The Caveats
No real‑world hardware deployments, performance benchmarks, or baseline comparisons are presented.

## NHTSA orders autonomous vehicle firms to stop blocking first responders

### The Breakthrough
NHTSA directed autonomous‑vehicle developers to add reliable detection of flashing lights, fire, smoke, cones and other emergency cues, making emergency‑scene handling a functional safety requirement in upcoming vehicle‑safety standards.

### The Caveats
The directive provides no concrete evaluation criteria or independent verification standards, leaving its practical impact unclear.

## New Jersey robotaxi law would require lidar, potentially banning Tesla's camera‑only fleet

### The Breakthrough
Proposed legislation would mandate a three‑sensor suite (camera, lidar, radar) and a three‑year pilot with 50 k supervised miles before removing safety drivers, which could bar Tesla’s camera‑only robotaxis in the state.

### The Caveats
The proposal offers no empirical evidence that multi‑sensor hardware improves safety or is feasible for deployment.

### Priority
Because GeoGS‑SLAM delivers a storage‑efficient dense mapping method that can immediately improve perception pipelines for monocular robots, it offers the most actionable hardware‑agnostic advance today.

### One action
Integrate the GeoGS pipeline into your monocular SLAM stack or prototype a lightweight dense mapping module to test the reported 80% storage reduction.
